channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Natasha installs free internet at Kogi federal college
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Senator Natasha Akpoti-Uduaghan facilitates free campus-wide Wi-Fi at Federal College of Education, Okene, enhancing learning and digital access for studen
Read More: https://punchng.com/natasha-installs-free-internet-at-kogi-federal-college/

item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Lagos State Government warns against circulating misleading flood images, accusing influencers of politicising environmental issues. Verify information bef
Read More: https://punchng.com/lagos-govt-accuses-influencers-of-circulating-false-flood-images/

item.title: Cash transfers to poor Nigerians FG’s legal duty, not charity —Falana
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Femi Falana declares cash transfers and social protection for poor Nigerians are a legal obligation under the NSIPA Act, not charity. Learn why the FG must
Read More: https://punchng.com/cash-transfers-to-poor-nigerians-fgs-legal-duty-not-charity-falana/

item.title: Police foil bandit attack in Sokoto, kill one, recover AK-47
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Sokoto State Police Command foiled a bandit attack in Daraye Village, Wamako LGA, killing one suspect and recovering an AK-47 rifle.
Read More: https://punchng.com/police-foil-bandit-attack-in-sokoto-kill-one-recover-ak-47/

item.title: Send in your questions for Tash Farrant ahead of Women's T20 World Cup final LIVE!
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football

item.title: Iranians pray for slain supreme leader Khamenei in son’s absence
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Iranians gather for slain Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei’s funeral, with his son Mojtaba conspicuously absent. Thousands mourn in Tehran, demanding justice.
Read More: https://punchng.com/iranians-pray-for-slain-supreme-leader-khamenei-in-sons-absence/

item.title: British GP: F1 drivers in CHAOTIC Lego cars race before 3pm main event LIVE!
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football

item.title: Don’t exhaust yourselves trying to please women, Pastor Funke Adejumo tells married men
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Pastor Funke Adejumo advises married men not to exhaust themselves trying to please women, stating no man can fully satisfy a woman’s desire for change.
Read More: https://punchng.com/dont-exhaust-yourselves-trying-to-please-women-pastor-funke-adejumo-tells-married-men/

item.title: Compassion for Ogun people prompted my defection to PDP — Iyabo Obasanjo
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Iyabo Obasanjo-Bello, daughter of ex-President Obasanjo, explains her defection from APC to PDP, citing compassion for Ogun people and alignment with PDP’s
Read More: https://punchng.com/compassion-for-ogun-people-prompted-my-defection-to-pdp-iyabo-obasanjo/

item.title: 2027 campaign must be issue-based, not personal attacks – Lamido to PDP candidates
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Former Jigawa State Governor and founding member of the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP), Sule Lamido, has called on aspiring politicians and candidates to conduct issue-based campaigns and avoid personal attacks as preparations for the 2027 general elections gather momentum. Lamido made the call Satu

item.title: FG denies spending ₦8tn outside budget, says IMF report misrepresented

item.description:
The Federal Government denies spending ₦8 trillion outside the 2026 budget, stating IMF report was misrepresented. Finance Minister Taiwo Oyedele clarifies

item.description: The National Association of Nigerian Students (NANS), Joint Campus Council, Ogun State Axis, has called on the management of Ogun State Institute of Technology (OGITECH) to conclude the Students’ Union Government election and declare the duly elected winners without further delay. The post NANS Ogun

item.title: Emotional Moment As Nigeria’s Youngest Chartered Accountant, 16, Meets Pastor Olukoya
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: There were tears, smiles and heartfelt words of gratitude as 16-year-old Danielle Osasere Clement-Egberanmwen, celebrated as Nigeria’s youngest chartered accountant, met with the General Overseer of Mountain of Fire and Miracles Ministries (MFM), Dr. Daniel Olukoya. In an emotional clip making

item.title: VIDEO: Tony Elumelu spotted buying roasted corn from roadside vendor
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Nigerian businessman and philanthropist Tony Elumelu has been seen in a video buying roasted corn from a roadside vendor. The clip showed Elumelu stopping to patronise the seller and also taking a bite of the roasted corn during the encounter. The video surfaced on social media a week after Nigeria’

item.title: Anambra APC Crisis Deepens As Factional Exco Moves To Take Over Secretariat
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The lingering leadership crisis in the Anambra State chapter of the All Progressives Congress (APC) deepened on Sunday following the attempted takeover of the party’s state secretariat in Awka by the factional executive led by Chief Ifeanyi Osegbo. However, the executive led by Senator Emma An

item.title: ‘US will be paying close attention to Nigeria’s 2027 election’ —Congressman
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: A member of the United States House of Representatives, Riley Moore, has said the administration of President Donald Trump will closely monitor how Nigeria conducts its 2027 general elections. The post ‘US will be paying close attention to Nigeria’s 2027 election’ —Congressman appeared f

item.title: BREAKING: IMF report: Tinubu’s govt breaks silence on alleged missing N8trn
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Federal Government has dismissed claims that over ₦8 trillion was spent outside the 2025 budget, insisting that all public expenditures were made within Nigeria’s constitutional and legal framework. In a statement issued on Sunday by Taiwo Oyedele, Minister of Finance, the govern

item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Indonesian troops have recovered the body of American pilot Nicholas Goselin from the restive Papua region.

item.title: Sam Larry Hospitalized Following Fatal Road Accident in Lagos
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Nigerian music promoter and socialite Sam Larry has been hospitalised following a severe automobile accident along the Lagos-Calabar Coastal Road in Lagos.   The incident occurred during the early hours of Saturday when his vehicle reportedly rammed into a heavy-duty trailer. The collision resu

item.title: Toke Makinwa drops blunt relationship advice on managing expectations
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Toke also argued that prior to the popular belief, money does not change people, it just reveals their true personality.

The company disclosed this during the graduation ceremony of the first cohort of the EngineerHer Programme, a one-year initiative designed to equip female grad

item.title: NPFL: Rangers boss Ilechukwu to undergo UEFA B Licence course

item.description: Rangers technical adviser, Fidelis Ilechukwu is set to begin his UEFA B Licence coaching course. The Flying Antelopes tactician arrived Glasgow, Scotland this week to undertake his professional development coaching programme. The course will boost the young tactician’s football knowledge. The

item.title: LPDC sets date to hear misconduct petition against SAN, 2 others
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: In his ruling, the chairman rejected the application, stating that the committee’s rules did not permit the withdrawal of petitions once issues had been joined.

item.title: Nigerian Woman Completes 144-Hour Bible Reading Marathon In Guinness World Record Attempt
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Nigerian woman, Habibat Salawudeen Ihiovi-Jack, has completed an extraordinary 144-hour Bible reading marathon in a bid to set a new Guinness World Record, drawing admiration for her endurance, discipline and unwavering devotion to Scripture. The marathon began on June 22, 2026, and saw Habibat spen

item.title: I will not demolish without paying compensation – Uba Sani
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Governor Uba Sani has vowed that his administration will never dispossess any citizen of Kaduna State his or her property, without fair and adequate compensation. The Governor made the pledge after distributing cheques to affected households along the 15 kilometre Ring Road corridor from Danbushiya

item.title: Why Kunsela, Ologolo, Gbagada and other parts of Lagos keep flooding
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: For many Lagos residents, flooding has become an all-too-familiar consequence of the rainy season. Yet the latest incidents have revived a familiar question: Why does Lagos continue to flood? The post Why Kunsela, Ologolo, Gbagada and other parts of Lagos keep flooding appeared first on Nairametrics

item.title: Israel appoints first-ever resident ambassador to Slovenia
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Ties between Israel and Slovenia had soured under the previous centre-left government of prime minister Robert Golob, which had characterised Israel's military offensive in Gaza as "genocide". The post Israel appoints first-ever resident ambassador to Slovenia appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: FG Begins N137bn Borno Highway Revival
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Federal Government has commenced the reconstruction of two major highways in Borno State with a combined value of N137 billion, in a move aimed at improving security, reviving commerce and reconnecting communities in Nigeria’s North-East. The projects, officially flagged off by Vice-Presid

item.title: England fighting history as well as Mexican nation in Azteca cauldron
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: England will be battling against history as well as entire nation when the face Mexico in the World Cup last 16 at The Azteca, says chief football writer Phil McNulty.

item.title: Brentford set to sign Burnley forward Anthony
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Brentford are nearing a move for Burnley forward Jaidon Anthony for a £15m fee rising to £17m with add-ons.

item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Emeka also noted that relationships in its natural form is sacred and should not be used as a means of manipulation and financial liberation.

item.title: Three vigilantes killed in night attack on Plateau community
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: According to community sources, the assailants stormed the community under the cover of darkness and targeted members of the local vigilante group who were on duty. The post Three vigilantes killed in night attack on Plateau community appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.description: A chieftain of the All Progressives Congress, APC, Olatunbosun Oyintiloye, has described the National Youth Service Corps, NYSC, reform as a major step towards repositioning the scheme as a skills-driven platform for youth empowerment and national development. Oyintiloye speaking with journalists in

item.title: NDLEA arrests 67-year-old grandma with 13kg cocaine hidden in fake plantains
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: NDLEA arrests a 67-year-old woman with 13kg of cocaine disguised as plantains and nabs a suspected drug syndicate leader. The post NDLEA arrests 67-year-old grandma with 13kg cocaine hidden in fake plantains appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Jailed Gaza hospital chief in life-threatening condition, rights group says
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Elyas Abu Safia says his father Hussam can barely breathe or speak after more than 555 days in Israeli prison.

item.title: Tears, And Triumph: The Rise Of Nigerian Women On The Global Music Stage
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Nigerian women have always risen to global stardom through hard work, determination and grace, despite the challenges they face. BUKOLA OGUNSINA and SAMUEL ABULUDE write about the impact of Nigerian female musicians on the global stage. With Afrobeats gaining traction worldwide, Nigerian female arti

item.title: Venezuela quakes final death toll expected to be 'much higher'
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Venezuela's devastating twin earthquakes killed nearly 3,000, updated official figures showed on Saturday, as international rescue teams began winding down search operations for survivors in the rubble of the disaster. FRANCE 24's Maxime Pluvinet reports from Caracas.

item.title: Prof Aghaji’s arrest proof Nnamdi Kanu’s ‘persecution’ politically motivated – Lawyer
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: A lawyer and public policy analyst, Barrister Christopher Chidera, has described the arrest of a medical expert, Emeritus Professor Martin Aghaji, as proof that the “persecution” of leader of the Indigenous People of Biafra, IPOB, Nnamdi Kanu, is politically motivated rather than a legal

item.title: Paris appeals court ruling could upend Marine Le Pen’s 2027 presidential bid
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Paris' appeals court is set to rule Tuesday in Marine Le Pen’s embezzlement case, a decision that could determine whether one of France’s leading presidential contenders can run in next year’s election.

item.title: NDLEA arrests 67-year-old grandmother over cocaine hidden in fake plantain
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The National Drug Law Enforcement Agency (NDLEA) has arrested a 67-year-old Nigerian-British grandmother, Mrs Mary Barek, for allegedly attempting to smuggle 13 kilograms of cocaine hidden inside fake plantain through The post NDLEA arrests 67-year-old grandmother over cocaine hidden in fake plantai

item.title: Zambia: Zambia's 2026 Election - Performance, Pressure, and Political Reality
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [allAfrica] As Zambia moves toward its 2026 general election, the contest is increasingly being framed not as a clash of ideologies, but as a national assessment of performance, expectations, and credibility.

item.title: US celebrates 250 years of independence amid polarising political landscape
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: The 250th anniversary of American independence is colliding with a country gripped by political polarisation and a heat wave bearing down on millions of people across multiple states as celebrations got underway Saturday throughout the United States.

item.title: Taraba CJ welcomes establishment of State Police
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: “I want to believe that this policy will go along way in maintaining law and order within local communities."

item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Nigeria’s rising sprint star, Kanyinsola Ajayi, claimed the biggest win of his career on Saturday after defeating reigning world champion Oblique Seville of Jamaica to win the men’s 100m at the 2026 Prefontaine Classic in Eugene, Oregon. Making his Diamond League debut at the renowned Ha

item.title: Sooryavanshi sets record for youngest India player in international match
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi became the youngest debutant for India in a T20 loss to England at Old Trafford.

item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Exercises from Monday to July 13 off Qingdao to be followed by joint maritime patrols in the Pacific Ocean.

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The palace of Adeyeye Ogunwusi, the Ooni of Ife, has dismissed reports that the controversial Nollywood actor, Olanrewaju Omiyinka, popularly known as Baba Ijesha, was conferred with a chieftaincy title. The clarification comes after the actor, on Friday, shared photographs of himself and his wife k

item.title: NIS Graduates 62 Senior Officers, Reaffirms Commitment To Leadership Development
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Nigeria Immigration Service (NIS) has reaffirmed its commitment to leadership development and institutional capacity building following the graduation of 62 senior officers from its 13th Command Course. The development was disclosed in a statement signed by the Service Public Relations Officer,

item.title: World Cup 2026: Morocco make history after thrashing Canada
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Morocco on Saturday made history after thrashing Canada 3-0 in the World Cup Round of 16 encounter at the Houston Stadium. A brace from Azzedine Ounahi and a goal from Soufiane Rahimi gave the Atlas Lions the win against the co-host nation. The win against Canada means Morocco becomes the

item.title: Release Dr Hussam Abu Safia immediately!
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: It’s been more than 1,000 days of genocide and 500 days of Abu Safia’s detention. We need urgent action to end both.

item.title: OPEC+ raises quotas again as Middle East calms
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Seven OPEC+ members decided on Sunday to again raise oil production quotas as Gulf countries reel from the Middle East war. The post OPEC+ raises quotas again as Middle East calms appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: Bishop Funke Felix-Adejumo has advised married men against exhausting themselves in a bid to satisfy their wives, stressing that no one can ever completely please a woman. Speaking during a sermon, the cleric urged men to remain responsible but warned against sacrificing their health and lives in th

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Nigeria has once again emerged as Africa’s leading beneficiary of the European Union’s Erasmus Mundus Joint Master’s scholarship programme, with 163 Nigerian students admitted this year and 89 securing fully funded Erasmus Mundus scholarships. item.description: Veteran Nollywood actor, Emeka Ike, has described dating for financial benefits as “romantic scam”. He claimed that some ladies go into relationships with the primary aim of raising funds, which according to him, equates to fraud. Speaking in a recent interview with BBC Pidgin, the actor

item.title: Taraba Chief Judge Backs Establishment Of State Police
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Chief Judge of Taraba State, Justice Joel Agya, has welcomed moves by President Bola Tinubu to establish state police, describing the initiative as a bold step towards addressing Nigeria’s worsening security challenges. Agya spoke in Wukari during a dinner and fundraising for the Justice J

item.dc:creator: Soccernet NG
item.description: Serie A clubs Bologna and Fiorentina have joined the race to sign Super Eagles winger Samuel Chukwueze this summer amid uncertainty over his future at AC Milan, Soccernet.ng reports. Chukwueze has become the subject of intense transfer speculation this summer following his return to the San Siro aft

item.title: The week in pictures: America at 250, Taylor Swift gets married and a 'massive attack' on Kyiv
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: America marks 250 years of independence under a heatwave, a massive Russian attack sets Kyiv ablaze, France dominates Sweden at the World Cup, and Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce get married at Madison Square Garden: FRANCE 24 looks back at some of the week's most striking images.

item.description: The federal government has condemned the killing of two Nigerian nationals in South Africa, calling on the South African authorities to urgently investigate the incidents and ensure those responsible are brought to justice. The government also expressed concern over what it described as a growing pa

item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Rep. Moore made this known in an interview with Noire TV while respondin

item.title: NBA Urges Lawyers To Champion Solutions To Nigeria’s Challenges
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Nigerian Bar Association (NBA), Gboko Branch, has called on lawyers to take a leading role in advancing practical solutions to Nigeria’s persistent challenges while upholding the rule of law and defending justice. The chairman of the NBA Gboko Branch, Felix Zahemen Uwar, made the call in h

item.title: WAFCON 2026: Malawi appoint technical adviser ahead Super Falcons clash
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Football Association Malawi, FAM, has appointed Sudesh Singh technical adviser to the Scorchers ahead of the 2026 Women’s Africa Cup of Nations, DAILY POST reports. Singh will work alongside head coach Lovemore Fazili and performance analyst Tyrese Steyn. According to FAM, the move follows

item.title: Lawyer Condemns Arrest Of Doctor Over Nnamdi Kanu’s Health Assessment
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: A public policy analyst, Barrister Christopher Chidera, has condemned the reported arrest of Emeritus Professor Martin Aghaji, describing it as an assault on truth, the rule of law and professional independence over the doctor’s medical assessment of detained Indigenous People of Biafra (IPOB)

item.title: Klopp Agrees To Become Germany Head Coach
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Jürgen Klopp has agreed to become the new head coach of the German national team after accepting the offer, according to football transfer expert Fabrizio Romano. Romano disclosed the development in a post on his official X page on Sunday, stating that discussions are still ongoing regarding the det

item.title: Anambra: How brake failure cost me my leg, planned wedding — Hairstylist
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Loveth Sunday, has shared how a road accident caused by a commercial bus with failed brakes led to the loss of her left leg and ended her plans to get married after The post Anambra: How brake failure cost me my leg, planned wedding — Hairstylist appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: As Tanzania’s crisis bites, President Samia closes ranks
item.dc:creator: The Africa Report
item.description: Fighting off western sanctions, President Samia Suluhu Hassan is promoting ultra-loyalists and discarding moderates in the new power elite.

item.title: Eno Targets Food Security, Agro-Tourism, Transfers Ibom Model Farm To Aksu
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Determined to boost food security and agro-tourism, Akwa Ibom State Governor, Pastor Umo Eno, has handed over the management of Ibom Model Farm to Akwa Ibom State University (AKSU). The Governor during an inspection of the facility explained that the government was in the advanced stage of providing

item.title: Air Chief Tasks Personnel On Research, Professionalism
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Chief of the Air Staff (CAS), Air Marshal Sunday Kelvin Aneke, has charged personnel of the Nigerian Air Force (NAF) to embrace research, strategic communication and professionalism as essential pillars for building a modern, innovative and mission-ready force capable of meeting the country&#821

item.title: Horner makes first return to F1 paddock and reveals what would tempt new role
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Christian Horner has made his first appearance in the Formula 1 paddock since his exit from Red Bull last year at Sunday's British Grand Prix.

item.title: NDLEA Arrests UK-Based Grandma With 13kg Cocaine Hidden in Plantain Peels
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Operatives of the National Drug Law Enforcement Agency (NDLEA) have arrested a 67-year-old Nigerian-British woman, Mary Yetunde Barek, after allegedly discovering 13 kilograms of cocaine concealed inside fake plantain peels at the Murtala Muhammed International Airport (MMIA), Lagos. The arrest was

item.title: Reform UK’s Farage failed to disclose funds from convicted criminal: Report
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: George Cottrell provided funds for Reform UK leader's security, drivers, staff and accommodation, Sunday Times reports.

item.title: World Cup: Atiku Congratulates Morocco On Quarter-Final Qualification
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The presidential candidate of the African Democratic Congress (ADC) in the forthcoming 2027 general elections, Atiku Abubakar, has congratulated Morocco on its qualification for the quarter-finals of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, describing the achievement as a proud moment for Africa. New Telegraph repo

item.title: SERAP demands National Assembly probe ₦1.3bn allocation to ‘fictitious’ presidential council
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: SERAP urges the National Assembly to investigate a ₦1.3bn allocation to an alleged council the Presidency says does not exist. The post SERAP demands National Assembly probe ₦1.3bn allocation to ‘fictitious’ presidential council appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Army confident of rescuing abducted Oyo schoolchildren, teachers – Shaibu
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Chief of Army Staff, Lt.-Gen. Waidi Shaibu, has expressed confidence that troops will safely rescue the 39 pupils and seven teachers abducted from schools in Oyo State, saying military operations are making steady progress. The post Army confident of rescuing abducted Oyo schoolchildren, teacher
